content.full_text:
April 19, 1976

Mr. Bert Johnson
3502 Surfwood Road
Malibu, California 90265

Dear Mr. Johnson:

This letter confirms our recent telephone conversation concerning the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. You are a chairman of a committee of Sunset Mesa Reform Act, another group of homeowners who wish to be excluded from the proposed incorporation of Malibu. Another group of homeowners want to hire you as a consultant to provide information that they will use in opposition to the proposed incorporation. You asked whether the opposition to the proposed incorporation would conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act limitation only to "public officials", that is a person who are members, officers, employees, or consultants of state or local government agencies. Government Code Sections, 87100, 82048. From the facts as I understand them, you are not a public official and are therefore not subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the Act.

At some future date, you may incur certain responsibilities in connection with the campaign disclosure provisions of the Act. After the incorporation proposal has qualified to be placed on the ballot, the provision is a measure as defined in the Act. Your organization will be required to file campaign statements if you collect $500 or more for in connection with the election if you collect $500 or more for in connection with the election in support of or opposition to the purpose of influencing voters in support of or opposition to the measure.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West
Staff Counsel
